message.content:
你是 correctness 審查者。審查目標：整體檢視 telegram-kiro-bridge 的 src/ 程式碼是否有問題（correctness / security / 資源洩漏）。
重點：邏輯錯誤 / 邊界條件 / 因果鏈漏接（改了 X 但連動的 Y 沒改——grep 呼叫者窮舉驗證）/ 設計-實作一致性。
對每個「改了某處」的判斷，用 grep 驗證呼叫者，呼叫者=0 的修改要警示。

attachment.content:
- claude-mem-curate: 把 claude-mem 抽出的 shortlist 精選成少量繁體中文、去重後寫進 AIMemory。當使用者說「精選/整理 claude-mem」「把最近重點存記憶」,或每日自動 ingestion 時使用。
- dual-skill-review-loop: Use when producing any deliverable that needs quality assurance — writing, reports, proposals, code designs — and you want the output to reach ≥95 score through structured self-review iterations
- excel-to-ai-doc: Convert image-heavy Excel game spec spreadsheets (.xlsx) into an AI-readable knowledge structure — Markdown text + extracted original images (anchored to their source cells) + metadata.json. Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.
- huashu-slides: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。
- knowhow-accumulation: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes
- memory-to-skill: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用
- ms-agent-scheduled-prompts: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用
- ms-agent-text-token-signaling: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用
- ms-external-repo-absorption: ms-external-repo-absorption
- ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用
- ms-portable-skill-authoring: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用
- ms-streaming-token-pipeline: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.
- ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.
- ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用
- ms-wiki-knowledge-base: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用
- ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用
- ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用
- non-engineer-agent-design: Use when designing Custom GPT / Claude Project / Gemini Gem instructions for non-engineers, or when helping someone structure their AI workflow using the 5-piece framework (Agent, Skill, Steering, Hook, KnowHow)
- self-eval-prompt-pattern: Use when designing prompts or instructions that require AI to self-assess quality before delivering output, when setting up automated quality gates in Custom GPT / Project / Gem instructions, or when AI outputs need measurable pass/fail criteria
- skill-creator: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.
- sync-fork-from-upstream: 當使用者要把自己的 fork / 下游倉庫同步到原專案（upstream）的最新更新時使用。觸發語包含「同步原專案」「拿上游更新」「sync upstream」「更新原專案」「原本的倉庫有更新怎麼拉」。涵蓋一次性 upstream remote 設定、半自動同步流程（fetch → 拋棄式分支試合併 → 型別檢查 → 停在衝突/失敗、絕不自動 push）、以及 fork 與 upstream 無共同歷史時的「重新接血脈」做法。
- ui-ux-pro-max: ui-ux-pro-max
- uk-slot-codegen: UK slot codegen 統一入口。使用者提供規格路徑+目標專案路徑，自動串接 adapter→codegen→validation 全流程。支援新專案/增量更新/僅驗證三種模式，每步 checkpoint 可中斷恢復。
- uk-slot-extrabet: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。
- uk-slot-fake-reel-manager: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充
- uk-slot-multilang-sync: 把規格 xlsx 的多國語言文字批次同步進 uk 線上老虎機框架各語系的 gameStrings.xml。當使用者要在老虎機專案新增/更新多國語言字串、提到 「gameStrings.xml」「多國語言」「多語系文字」「i18n 字串」「把 xlsx 的翻譯填進去」、 或要為某個 Key 一次寫入多國(常見 26 國)文字時使用。僅適用線上部門 uk 框架 (plist 格式 gameStrings.xml、每語系一目錄、欄標題括號內為語系代碼); 商用部門或其他文字格式不適用。
- uk-slot-pattern-library: uk-slot-pattern-library
- uk-slot-project-docs: uk-slot-project-docs
- uk-slot-spec-to-impl: 當拿到一份 UK 老虎機遊戲規格書（xlsx）要開發新遊戲、使用者說「從規格書實作」「開新的 slot 專案」「規格書轉開發規格」、或要為新遊戲起專案/拆任務時使用。涵蓋 xlsx 轉換、差異開發規格、milestone 任務拆解三步驟。
- uk-slot-state-machine: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。
- vc-kiro-delegate: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。
- writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- dev:init-claude: dev:init-claude — 開發設計閉環初始化
- sc:analyze: Comprehensive code analysis across quality, security, performance, and architecture domains
- sc:brainstorm: Interactive requirements discovery through Socratic dialogue and systematic exploration
- sc:build: Build, compile, and package projects with intelligent error handling and optimization
- sc:business-panel: /sc:business-panel - Business Panel Analysis System
- sc:cleanup: Systematically clean up code, remove dead code, and optimize project structure
- sc:design: Design system architecture, APIs, and component interfaces with comprehensive specifications
- sc:document: Generate focused documentation for components, functions, APIs, and features
- sc:estimate: Provide development estimates for tasks, features, or projects with intelligent analysis
- sc:explain: Provide clear explanations of code, concepts, and system behavior with educational clarity
- sc:git: Git operations with intelligent commit messages and workflow optimization
- sc:help: List all available /sc commands and their functionality
- sc:implement: Feature and code implementation with intelligent persona activation and MCP integration
- sc:improve: Apply systematic improvements to code quality, performance, and maintainability
- sc:index: Generate comprehensive project documentation and knowledge base with intelligent organization
- sc:load: Session lifecycle management with Serena MCP integration for project context loading
- sc:reflect: Task reflection and validation using Serena MCP analysis capabilities
- sc:save: Session lifecycle management with Serena MCP integration for session context persistence
- sc:select-tool: Intelligent MCP tool selection based on complexity scoring and operation analysis
- sc:spawn: Meta-system task orchestration with intelligent breakdown and delegation
- sc:spec-panel: Multi-expert specification review and improvement using renowned specification and software engineering experts
- sc:task: Execute complex tasks with intelligent workflow management and delegation
- sc:test: Execute tests with coverage analysis and automated quality reporting
- sc:troubleshoot: Diagnose and resolve issues in code, builds, deployments, and system behavior
- sc:workflow: Generate structured implementation workflows from PRDs and feature requirements
- uk-conventions: 把 UK 老虎機開發規範（禁改公版 + 命名規則）寫入當前專案的 CLAUDE.md（冪等，不重複貼）
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dev-design: 架構設計 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 1+1b）：多方案 judge-panel → 對抗驗證砍缺陷 → 設計規格（含 BC-x）。承重核（因果鏈+事實求證）注入每個 agent。
- dev-prd: PRD / 需求探索 workflow（新能力，原方法論無 PRD 階段）：多角度需求探索（problem/user/scope）→ 塑形候選（lean/complete）→ 對抗挑戰 → PRD 文件。事實求證確保需求前提不是憑空假設。
- dev-review: 品質+安全審查 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 3）：parallel 多視角審查（correctness/security/repro lens）→ 對抗驗證 findings。異源 skeptic 打破同源自審天花板（#007）。
- dev-verify: 跨產出物自證 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 5，可選）：可枚舉項用 adversarial-verify；反向遍歷（找死碼/未實作）用輕量 verifier agent（這是 adversarial-verify 結構上蓋不到的缺口）。
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-mem:do: Execute a plan using subagents for implementation
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create an implementation plan with documentation discovery
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- webwright:craft: Craft a reusable Webwright CLI tool by parameterizing a web task.
- webwright:run: Run a one-shot web task with the Webwright Playwright workflow.
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring skill invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- webwright:webwright: Solve a user-specified web task code-as-action style by driving a local Playwright browser through one bash command at a time, saving screenshots and an action log into `final_runs/run_<id>/`, and visually verifying the result. Use when the user asks to automate a web task (search, filter, form-fill, multi-step flow, data extraction) and wants reusable scripts plus screenshot evidence rather than a one-shot answer.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:a11y-debugging: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP for accessibility (a11y) debugging and auditing based on web.dev guidelines. Use when testing semantic HTML, ARIA labels, focus states, keyboard navigation, tap targets, and color contrast.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:chrome-devtools: Uses Chrome DevTools via MCP for efficient debugging, troubleshooting and browser automation. Use when debugging web pages, automating browser interactions, analyzing performance, or inspecting network requests. This skill does not apply to `--slim` mode (MCP configuration).
- chrome-devtools-mcp:chrome-devtools-cli: Use this skill to write shell scripts or run shell commands to automate tasks in the browser or otherwise use Chrome DevTools via CLI.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:debug-optimize-lcp: Guides debugging and optimizing Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) using Chrome DevTools MCP tools. Use this skill whenever the user asks about LCP performance, slow page loads, Core Web Vitals optimization, or wants to understand why their page's main content takes too long to appear. Also use when the user mentions "largest contentful paint", "page load speed", "CWV", or wants to improve how fast their hero image or main content renders.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:memory-leak-debugging: Diagnoses and resolves memory leaks in JavaScript/Node.js applications. Use when a user reports high memory usage, OOM errors, or wants to analyze heapsnapshots or run memory leak detection tools like memlab.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:troubleshooting: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP and documentation to troubleshoot connection and target issues. Trigger this skill when list_pages, new_page, or navigate_page fail, or when the server initialization fails.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	// Goal state store — AB-5.
2	//
3	// Persists per-chat /goal state so it survives bridge restarts and
4	// idle-session drops. Schema documented in docs/ab-token-protocol.md §6.2.
5	//
6	// State machine:
7	//
8	//   (no file) --/goal text-->           active
9	//   active    --GOAL_DONE-->            done
10	//   active    --budget reached-->       paused
11	//   active    --/goal pause-->          paused
12	//   active    --/goal clear-->          cleared
13	//   paused    --/goal resume-->         active (turns_used reset to 0)
14	//   paused    --/goal clear-->          cleared
15	//   done      --/goal clear-->          cleared
16	//   done      --/goal <new text>-->     active (new objective)
17	//   cleared   --/goal <new text>-->     active (new objective)
18	//
19	// Rejection rules:
20	//   - set() while status='active' or 'paused' → thrown error; caller
21	//     must /stop or /goal clear first.
22	//   - pause/resume/clear/markDone on missing file → thrown error.
23	//   - incrementTurn on non-active goal → thrown error.
24	//
25	// File layout:
26	//   ${MEMORY_DIR}/goal-state/<chat_id>.json
27	//
28	// Concurrency: serialized via atomicJson per file path — each chat's
29	// goal state file has its own independent chain.
30	
31	import path from "node:path";
32	import {
33	  atomicWriteJson,
34	  readJsonOrDefault,
35	  updateJson,
36	} from "./atomicJson.js";
37	
38	export type GoalStatus = "active" | "paused" | "done" | "cleared";
39	
40	export interface GoalState {
41	  _schema_version: 1;
42	  chat_id: number;
43	  status: GoalStatus;
44	  objective: string;
45	  turns_used: number;
46	  max_turns: number;
47	  started_at: string;       // ISO
48	  last_turn_at: string | null;
49	  last_reason: string | null;
50	  created_by: number | null; // user id, optional
51	  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52	  // RESTART token guardrail fields (C3, 2026-05-02).
53	  //
54	  // Agent can emit <<RESTART:reason>> to ask the bridge to restart
55	  // the Node process mid-goal (to reload code / env / MCP servers).
56	  // These three fields track how many times this goal has used that
57	  // lever, when the last one happened, and what reasons were given —
58	  // so the guardrail in runPrompt can enforce cooldown + per-goal
59	  // cap and so the user can later review why a goal burned 3 restarts.
60	  //
61	  // Legacy state files written before this schema extension will be
62	  // missing these fields; get() normalises them to defaults on read,
63	  // so old state continues to work without a migration write.
64	  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	  /** ISO timestamp of the last successful RESTART triggered by this goal, or null. */
66	  last_restart_at: string | null;
67	  /** How many times RESTART has fired during this goal's lifetime. */
68	  restart_count: number;
69	  /** Reasons supplied by the agent for each restart, oldest first. */
70	  restart_reasons: string[];
71	  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72	  // Reflexion retry fields (5c, 2026-05-20).
73	  //
74	  // When a goal exhausts its turn budget without GOAL_DONE, the bridge
75	  // can auto-retry with a reflection prompt up to GOAL_REFLECTION_MAX
76	  // times. Each retry resets turns_used and injects a reflection hint.
77	  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78	  /** How many reflection retries have been attempted for this goal. */
79	  reflection_retries: number;
80	  /** The last reflection summary injected before a retry, or null. */
81	  last_reflection: string | null;
82	}
83	
84	export const DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS = 5;
85	
86	/** Maximum number of reflection retries when a goal exhausts budget without GOAL_DONE. */
87	export const GOAL_REFLECTION_MAX = 3;
88	
89	// Guardrail constants for <<RESTART>> token (C3, 2026-05-02). Hard-coded
90	// on purpose — the user chose "default, no env var override; change here
91	// if it turns out to be wrong." See docs/ab-token-protocol.md §RESTART.
92	/** Minimum gap between two successful RESTARTs on the same goal. */
93	export const GOAL_RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
94	/** Maximum number of RESTARTs a single goal can fire. */
95	export const GOAL_RESTART_CAP_PER_GOAL = 5;
96	
97	/** Directory containing per-chat goal-state files. */
98	export function goalStateDir(memoryDir: string): string {
99	  return path.join(memoryDir, "goal-state");
100	}
101	
102	/** Path to the goal-state file for one chat. */
103	export function goalStatePath(memoryDir: string, chatId: number): string {
104	  return path.join(goalStateDir(memoryDir), `${chatId}.json`);
105	}
106	
107	function nowIso(): string {
108	  return new Date().toISOString();
109	}
110	
111	function errEmpty(chatId: number): Error {
112	  return new Error(`no goal state for chat ${chatId}`);
113	}
114	
115	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
116	// Read
117	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118	
119	/** Current goal state for this chat, or null if no file exists. Corrupt
120	 * files are treated as "no goal" (readJsonOrDefault returns null).
121	 *
122	 * Normalises legacy state files that pre-date the RESTART guardrail
123	 * fields (last_restart_at / restart_count / restart_reasons) by filling
124	 * in defaults on read. We intentionally do NOT write back here — the
125	 * next real mutation (incrementTurn / markRestart / etc.) will persist
126	 * the normalised shape, avoiding a write storm on bridge startup. */
127	export async function get(
128	  memoryDir: string,
129	  chatId: number,
130	): Promise<GoalState | null> {
131	  const raw = await readJsonOrDefault<GoalState | null>(
132	    goalStatePath(memoryDir, chatId),
133	    () => null,
134	  );
135	  if (!raw || typeof raw !== "object") return null;
136	  if (raw._schema_version !== 1) return null;
137	  // Legacy shapes (pre-2026-05-02) don't have the RESTART fields. Fill
138	  // in-memory so downstream callers see a consistent GoalState. Cast
139	  // through unknown so TS lets us poke at possibly-absent fields.
140	  const legacy = raw as unknown as Partial<GoalState>;
141	  return {
142	    ...raw,
143	    last_restart_at:
144	      typeof legacy.last_restart_at === "string" ? legacy.last_restart_at : null,
145	    restart_count:
146	      typeof legacy.restart_count === "number" && legacy.restart_count >= 0
147	        ? legacy.restart_count
148	        : 0,
149	    restart_reasons: Array.isArray(legacy.restart_reasons)
150	      ? legacy.restart_reasons.filter((s): s is string => typeof s === "string")
151	      : [],
152	    reflection_retries:
153	      typeof legacy.reflection_retries === "number" && legacy.reflection_retries >= 0
154	        ? legacy.reflection_retries
155	        : 0,
156	    last_reflection:
157	      typeof legacy.last_reflection === "string" ? legacy.last_reflection : null,
158	  };
159	}
160	
161	/** True iff the chat has a goal currently in 'active' state. */
162	export async function isActive(
163	  memoryDir: string,
164	  chatId: number,
165	): Promise<boolean> {
166	  const s = await get(memoryDir, chatId);
167	  return s?.status === "active";
168	}
169	
170	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
171	// Mutations — all go through updateJson() for atomic read-modify-write
172	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	
174	/** Start a new goal or overwrite a terminated one. Throws if the chat
175	 * already has an active or paused goal — caller must /goal clear first. */
176	export async function set(
177	  memoryDir: string,
178	  chatId: number,
179	  objective: string,
180	  opts: { maxTurns?: number; createdBy?: number } = {},
181	): Promise<GoalState> {
182	  const maxTurns = opts.maxTurns ?? DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS;
183	  if (maxTurns < 1) throw new Error(`maxTurns must be >= 1 (got ${maxTurns})`);
184	  const ts = nowIso();
185	
186	  // set() has special semantics: "(no file) → active" vs "active/paused
187	  // → throw" vs "done/cleared → overwrite". updateJson's read-modify-
188	  // write primitive doesn't fit — its `initial()` callback is how we'd
189	  // represent "no file", but the `update()` callback then receives that
190	  // initial value and can't tell it apart from a real active goal.
191	  //
192	  // So we do: read current state, apply rejection logic outside the
193	  // write chain, then atomicWriteJson the new state.
194	  const existing = await get(memoryDir, chatId);
195	  if (
196	    existing &&
197	    (existing.status === "active" || existing.status === "paused")
198	  ) {
199	    throw new Error(
200	      `chat ${chatId} already has a ${existing.status} goal; ` +

findings.problem: _cancelledTurn 是「寫入者=1、讀取者=0」的斷鏈欄位（grep 全 src 只有宣告與 cancel() 內賦值兩處命中）。註解宣稱「run-prompt finally skips post-processing」，但 runPrompt 的 finally 與 post-processing 完全沒檢查它。cancel() 樂觀地把 inflight=false 後，使用者立刻發新訊息開新 turn（resetTurn 清 buffer、設新 onUpdate）；被取消的舊 turn 的 client.prompt() 稍後以 stopReason=cancelled 返回，舊 runPrompt 繼續完整收尾：(a) 用「共享 session 物件上屬於新 turn 的 buffer/toolTimeline」做 final render 與 token 副作用（SCHEDULE/RELAY/SEND_FILE 可能被半成品輸出觸發），(b) history.push 污染，(c) finally 設 session.onUpdate=undefined 殺掉新 turn 的 streaming，(d) inflight=false 破壞並發閘門，允許第三個 prompt 同時進場。
findings.fix: 在 runPrompt 的 prompt() resolve 後（turnFinalized 前）與 finally 內檢查 session._cancelledTurn：命中則跳過所有 post-processing 與 delivery，且不覆寫 onUpdate/inflight（改為只在「session 仍屬於本 turn」時清理——可用 turn 序號/token 比對）。新 turn 開始（resetTurn）時清 _cancelledTurn。

findings.problem: get() 對同一 chatId 的並發呼叫沒有 in-progress 去重。create() 內有多個秒級 await（loadFacts、local-LLM compressFactsForPreamble、readFile、provider.initialize=spawn 子進程+RPC）。並發觸發點實際存在：message handler、ASK callback、specialist/parallel 結果注入（run-prompt.ts:1305/1417 的背景 .then）、pet chat、/back。兩個並發 get() → 各自 spawn 一個 ACP 子進程，後者 sessions.set 覆蓋前者；被覆蓋的 session 的子進程永遠不會被 close（exit handler 以 sessions.get(chatId)===session 判斷，覆蓋後恆 false 也不清理）→ 子進程與記憶體洩漏，且兩份 preamble 各注入一次。
findings.fix: 在 SessionManager 加 per-chatId 的 creating: Map<number, Promise<ChatSession>>；get() 發現無活 session 時先查 creating，有則 await 同一個 promise，無則登記 create() 的 promise 並在 settle 後刪除。

findings.problem: switchAgent/switchSession/newSession 都有 existing?.inflight 擋牆（同類掃描下 switchProvider 是唯一漏掉的）。/model 切換在 turn 進行中會直接 delete + close 舊 client：進行中的 runPrompt 的 prompt() 以 'ACP agent exited' reject，錯誤訊息噴給使用者，且舊 runPrompt 的 finally 繼續改「舊 session 物件」狀態；若使用者接著操作新 session，與 finding 1 同型的跨 turn 干擾。
findings.fix: switchProvider 開頭加上與 switchAgent 相同的 inflight 檢查：existing?.inflight 時 throw「目前有 turn 進行中，請先 /cancel」。

findings.problem: askRegistry 是無界 in-memory Map，唯一刪除點是 ASK 按鈕被點擊（index.ts:359）。runPrompt/orphan flush/proxy/relay 每次帶 keyboard 的訊息都 set（run-prompt.ts:255,267,1218；index.ts:1032,1042；commands/relay.ts:292,301），從未點擊的按鈕（多數情況）永久殘留。長駐 bridge 數週後穩定成長的記憶體洩漏；同時 bridge 重啟後點舊按鈕 registry 遺失只剩 key fallback（已知，非本項）。
findings.fix: 改為帶 TTL 的結構：set 時記 timestamp，用一個 unref 的定時 sweep（例如 24h 過期）或 LRU 上限（如 500 筆）淘汰最舊 entry。

findings.problem: telegram transport 路徑完全忽略 targetBot 參數，一律送到固定的 cfg.targetBotUsername；redis 路徑卻正確用 redisKey(targetBot)。runPrompt 解析出的 <<RELAY:peer|...>> target 是 agent 任意指定的多 peer 名稱——transport=telegram/both 時，寫給 peer B 的訊息會靜默誤投給設定裡唯一的 target bot，而 group mirror 卻顯示「-> ${targetBot}」，log 與實際投遞不一致，除錯時會被誤導。
findings.fix: 短期：telegram 路徑在 targetBot 與 cfg.targetBotUsername 對不上（去 @ 與大小寫正規化後）時警告並拒送/僅走 redis；長期：把 targetBotUsername 改為 per-peer map（peer 名 → bot username）。

findings.problem: close() 兩個問題：(1) 用 execSync('taskkill ...') 同步阻塞 event loop（預設無 timeout 上限的子行程呼叫，多 session shutdown 時串行阻塞所有 chat 的 streaming/HTTP）；(2) close() 不設 this.closed=true 也不 reject pending——若 taskkill 與 fallback kill 都沒生效（例如 pid 已被回收重用），pending 的 session/prompt promise 永遠懸掛，呼叫端 runPrompt 的 inflight 永不釋放（sweepIdle 的 health monitor 探測的是新 pid 持有者，可能誤判存活）。
findings.fix: close() 改 spawn 非同步 taskkill（或 execFile + await），並在發出 kill 後設 closed=true、以 'ACP agent closed' reject 所有 pending entries；exit event 到來時的既有清理保持冪等。

findings.problem: 修復用 regex /<<SEND_FILE:\s*([\s\S]+?)\s*>>/ 允許跨行匹配：一個未閉合的 <<SEND_FILE: opener 會貪到後文任意位置的下一個 '>>'（例如文中引用的 '>>' 或另一個殘缺 token），把中間所有換行改寫成字面 \n 後重組成單行 token，讓後續 SEND_FILE_RE 誤抓一條垃圾路徑、同時吞掉那段可見文字。
findings.fix: 把 [\s\S]+? 的跨行範圍限制為「至多 N 個換行 / 只允許緊跟在反斜線語境的控制字元」，或先逐行掃描、只修復同一 span 內確為 \r\n\t 反序列化產物的情況。

findings.problem: openerRe 白名單缺 MOA_PLAN、SPECIALIST_PROXY（以及 CONTINUE 無所謂）：agent 在 stream 中途寫到一半的 <<MOA_PLAN:{... 或 <<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|... 會原樣閃現在 streaming 幀與（stream 中斷時的）final render，違反此 helper 自述的 UX 保證——同類掃描漏接（新增 token 時沒同步更新這裡）。
findings.fix: openerRe 加入 MOA_PLAN|SPECIALIST_PROXY（與未來新 token 一起維護；可考慮由 token 前綴清單自動組 regex 避免再度漏接）。

findings.problem: 死碼：relayReplyTo 為真的路徑已在 1054 行無條件 return，因此 1378 行 if (relayReplyTo && ...) 的 auto relay reply 永遠不可達。閱讀者會誤以為 user-facing 路徑也會回覆 relay peer。
findings.fix: 刪除該分支，或加註解說明 relay 路徑已在上方提前返回（依 10.5 立場：屬既有死碼，僅提報不強制動）。

findings.problem: emotionVectors 首次呼叫即永久快取；若當時 embedText 對部分/全部 emotion 回 null（模型暖機中），缺席的向量永遠不會補算，甚至可能快取成空 Map，此後 sticker 自動選擇對這些情緒恆失效直到重啟。
findings.fix: 只有在全部 emotion 都成功取得向量時才寫入快取；部分失敗時保持 null 讓下次重試（或記錄缺失清單逐次補算）。

findings.problem: pinned-agent.json 以 fire-and-forget 載入；bridge 啟動後、載入完成前抵達的第一則訊息會用 .env 預設 agent 建 session，之後 pinned map 才補進來（has() 檢查不覆蓋，選擇正確），造成該 chat 靜默用錯 backend 直到 session 被 drop 重建。機率低但 /agent 使用者難以察覺。
findings.fix: create() 內解析 pinned backend 前 await 一個一次性的 pinnedLoaded promise（constructor 中建立），確保首次 session 建立時 pinned 資料已就緒。
